statement: Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party) has shifted on European integration over decades. In the early 1990s, the party leadership (under Davíð Oddsson) supported EEA membership as an alternative to EC/EU membership. By the 2000s, the party consolidated around an anti-EU but pro-EEA position. Following the 2008 crisis, an internal faction led by Bjarni Benediktsson (now party leader) briefly expressed openness to reconsidering the EU question, but the party's 2013 manifesto firmly rejected EU membership. As of 2025, the party opposes membership but has not whipped against the referendum process. An estimated 20–25% of Independence Party voters support EU membership, creating a significant internal tension that the party manages by focusing on fisheries and sovereignty arguments.
source_name: Sjálfstæðisflokkur — Party congresses and manifestos 1991–2025; Morgunblaðið archives
caveats: Internal party dynamics are complex and party congresses do not always reflect the views of the broader voter base. The party's position may evolve during the referendum campaign, particularly if business-oriented members push for a more nuanced stance.

related_entries.statement: Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party), Framsóknarflokkurinn (Progressive Party), Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) oppose EU membership. The Independence Party has historically been the strongest opponent, citing sovereignty and fisheries concerns. The Progressive Party, traditionally representing rural interests, opposes membership primarily over agricultural and fisheries policy. These four parties collectively held approximately 33–35 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

EU support varies dramatically by party affiliation. Among Viðreisn voters, ~90% support EU membership; among Samfylkingin voters, ~75%. Píratar voters show ~55% support, and Left-Green voters ~45%. On the opposing side, Sjálfstæðisflokkur voters show ~25% support, Framsóknarflokkur voters ~20%, Miðflokkur voters ~10%, and Flokkur fólksins voters ~10%. These figures (from late 2025 polling) suggest that the referendum will cut across party lines, with significant minorities within each party diverging from the official party position. The ~25% of Independence Party voters who support membership is particularly significant given that party's large vote share (~22% in recent elections).

citations.report_title: Að kljúfa þjóðina með tilfinningaþrunginni spurningu
Flott embætti í boði fyrir ESB ríki

claims.claim_text: Þingmenn Sjálfstæðisflokksins hafi lagt fram frumvarp um reglur um starfsval eftir ákveðin opinber embætti, þar sem lagt sé til 18 mánaða biðtími áður en embættismenn sem tóku þátt í aðildarviðræðum geti tekið við stöðum hjá gagnaðilanum.

citations.report_title: Landbúnaðarstefna ESB henti íslenskum landbúnaði illa

claims.claim_text: Formaður Sjálfstæðisflokksins sagði dagsetninguna henta "frasakenndri" stefnu ríkisstjórnarinnar en ekki þjóðinni.

citations.report_title: Snorri segir húsnæði verða jafndýrt þótt vextir lækki

claims.claim_text: Sjálfstæðisflokkur, Framsóknarflokkur og Flokkur fólksins leggjast gegn ESB-aðild ásamt Miðflokknum.
